name: trail
description: Main-flow return stack — `/trail` is a LIFO deviation stack that records where you were on the MAIN FLOW (the active goal) when you pivot into ANY side-task — an intra-repo tangent/sub-fix OR a cross-repo/upstream fix — so the parent goal is never stranded. Deviations are NOT only cross-repo; a same-repo tangent off the active milestone strands the main flow just as easily. The store is HOME-global (`~/.sidecar/trail.tape`) — NOT cwd-local like `/draft` — so one stack tracks the current dive across intra-repo deviations AND survives `cd` when a detour does cross repos (e.g. anima DECODER → hexa-lang flame → codegen fix). Verbs — `push <return-target>` appends a frame (UTC timestamp + git-toplevel repo basename + the return-target text) · `pop` removes the top (deepest) frame and prints the next frame to resume (or 'back on MAIN FLOW' when empty) · bare/`show` renders the ladder with the deepest frame marked `★ NOW` at top and the root at the bottom · `clear` wipes · `help`. LLM AUTO-USE (commons g74 companion) — SKILL.md instructs the calling LLM to run `/trail push` the instant work pivots into any side-task (intra-repo tangent/sub-fix or cross-repo/upstream fix) and `/trail pop` the instant it closes, with no explicit command, and to `/trail` (show) on long yak-shaves to re-surface the climb-back path. Distinct from `/draft` (cwd-local throwaway) and `/domain` (in-repo milestone SSOT). Portable POSIX script (head/tail/grep/awk, no GNU-only flags) so it runs identically on macOS and Linux pool hosts. Triggers: /trail · 트레일 · 복귀 스택 · 이탈 기록 · 메인 흐름 표시 · breadcrumb · return stack · 어디까지 했더라 · 원래 뭐 하던 · 곁가지 · detour · climb back · 본 흐름 복귀.
